metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify with 0734's actual output whether a `[metrics]` pip extra will exist or whether `scc`/`jscpd` are PATH-binary prerequisites only; if no extra is created, update AC1 and the reuse-surface.md section to describe external-CLI detection (e.g. `shutil.which`) rather than a pip extra name.
evidence: src/rebar/_optional.py EXTRAS dict contains only {agents, eval, tracing, grounding} — no 'metrics' key
pyproject.toml [project.optional-dependencies] has no 'metrics' entry
Ticket 0734 file_impact: git_metrics.py, registry.py, analyzer.py, test_metrics_cli.py — no pyproject.toml in scope

pyproject.toml comment for 'grounding' extra: 'The out-of-process backends (universal-ctags, OpenGrep/ast-grep, scc/lizard) are external CLIs, not pip deps' — suggesting scc is already framed as a PATH CLI, not a pip extra
findings.finding: AC1 specifies documenting 'the optional `[metrics]` extra + external CLIs (scc, jscpd via npx)' but no `[metrics]` extra exists in `pyproject.toml` or `_optional.py`'s `EXTRAS` dict, and ticket 0734 (the declared content prerequisite) does not appear to create one — its files-touched are `git_metrics.py`, `registry.py`, `analyzer.py`, `test_metrics_cli.py` with no `pyproject.toml` change. If the final shape from 0734 does not include a `[metrics]` pip extra (and scc/jscpd are instead PATH-binary prerequisites with no extra), documenting it as a pip extra would mislead adopters into running `pip install nava-rebar[metrics]` against a non-existent extra.
findings.location: AC line 1: 'docs/reuse-surface.md metrics section: analyzer seam + `[metrics]` extra + external CLIs'
scenarios: Implementer writes docs saying 'install nava-rebar[metrics]' but 0734 never creates that extra, so adopters get an install error
Implementer extends test_metrics_docs.py to assert '[metrics]' appears in reuse-surface.md, but the extra name is wrong — the test would pass against incorrect docs

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify whether a `[metrics]` pip extra is actually introduced by the dep chain (0734/21fb/3ba0) before documenting it; if scc and jscpd remain external PATH binaries (not a pip extra), replace `[metrics]` in AC1 with the correct description — e.g. 'no-extra required; scc and jscpd are external CLIs detected via PATH' — so the docs match the real install surface.
evidence: src/rebar/_optional.py EXTRAS dict: keys are 'agents', 'eval', 'tracing', 'grounding' — no 'metrics' key
pyproject.toml [project.optional-dependencies]: lists mcp, reviewbot, ui, agents, eval, tracing, grounding, grounding-t2, dev — no 'metrics' group
pyproject.toml line 142: 'The out-of-process backends (universal-ctags, OpenGrep/ast-grep, scc/lizard) are external CLIs, not pip deps'

Multiple sibling-ticket plan-review cache hits confirm: .rebar/cache/plan-review/a532-4e67-562c-4893/b03e180af14754c1520e.json, 999afb1685a87e4244af.json, dd13a9273fe8f4cf0acf.json, .rebar/cache/plan-review/21fb-f4cd-3eaa-4b60/d1c3075268e7c1e6196e.json all flag 'no metrics extra in _optional.EXTRAS'
Dep ticket 0734 (open) wires code_health readers to the analyzer — it does not create a [metrics] extra
findings.finding: AC1 names 'the optional `[metrics]` extra' as a documentable surface, but `src/rebar/_optional.py`'s EXTRAS dict contains only 'agents', 'eval', 'tracing', and 'grounding' — no 'metrics' entry — and `pyproject.toml` has no `[metrics]` optional-dependency group. scc and jscpd are external PATH binaries (not pip deps, per the pyproject.toml comment 'out-of-process backends … are external CLIs, not pip deps'). Documenting a `[metrics]` extra as if it exists would publish a false capability claim to adopters.
findings.location: AC1: 'docs/reuse-surface.md metrics section: analyzer seam + `[metrics]` extra + external CLIs'
scenarios: An adopter reads the new docs/reuse-surface.md metrics section and runs `pip install nava-rebar[metrics]`, getting an 'invalid extra' error from pip, breaking the install path the docs describe.
The test_metrics_docs.py extension asserts the docs mention the [metrics] extra; that assertion then encodes a false API contract that future pip-installable packages must replicate.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'optional `[metrics]` extra' in AC1 with the accurate framing: scc is an external PATH binary (fail-open when absent, same pattern as the grounding engine) and jscpd is invoked via `npx` (Node.js toolchain prerequisite). If the dep chain does introduce a `[metrics]` extra (i.e. if 21fb/3ba0 add `lizard` as a pip dep behind `guard_import(..., extra='metrics')`), the plan must first confirm that entry exists in `_optional.EXTRAS` before documenting it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether a real `[metrics]` pip extra will be added to pyproject.toml (with actual pip-installable packages) or whether scc/jscpd should be documented as system prerequisites (following the established pattern in the grounding extra comment) — the doc must match the actual install surface.
evidence: pyproject.toml: no [metrics] entry in [project.optional-dependencies]; existing extras (mcp, agents, grounding, etc.) all gate pip-installable packages
pyproject.toml grounding comment: 'The out-of-process backends (universal-ctags, OpenGrep/ast-grep, scc/lizard) are external CLIs, not pip deps'
Plan description: 'the optional `[metrics]` extra + external-CLI prerequisites (scc, jscpd via npx)'
ABSENCE: no metrics key in pyproject.toml [project.optional-dependencies]
findings.finding: The plan documents a `[metrics]` optional extra (AC: 'docs/reuse-surface.md metrics section: analyzer seam + `[metrics]` extra + external CLIs'), but no such extra exists in pyproject.toml, and the project's established pattern explicitly treats scc and other external CLIs as SYSTEM prerequisites — not pip extras. The grounding extra comment states 'The out-of-process backends (universal-ctags, OpenGrep/ast-grep, scc/lizard) are external CLIs, not pip deps.' Documenting a pip extra that gates non-pip system tools would cargo-cult the extras mechanism inconsistently.
findings.location: AC line 1: 'docs/reuse-surface.md metrics section: analyzer seam + `[metrics]` extra + external CLIs'
scenarios: A reader following the docs installs `pip install nava-rebar[metrics]` expecting scc/jscpd to be installed, but the extra doesn't exist — an InstallationError or silent skip.

The documented `[metrics]` extra implies pip-installable packages exist, but scc and jscpd/npx are Node/Go binaries; documenting them as a pip extra misleads adopters about the actual install mechanism.
findings.suggested_fix: If no pip packages are being gated, drop the `[metrics]` extra framing and document scc + jscpd/npx as system prerequisites (the same way `acli` is documented for Jira reconciliation), consistent with the grounding extra's treatment of 'external CLIs, not pip deps'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether the `[metrics]` pip extra will be created (in `pyproject.toml` and `_optional.py`) by a ticket in the 0734 dependency chain before this docs ticket ships, or remove the extra from AC1/Description if `scc`/`jscpd` are PATH-only prerequisites requiring no pip extra.
evidence: pyproject.toml extras: eval, tracing, grounding, agents — no 'metrics' key
src/rebar/_optional.py EXTRAS dict contains only: agents, eval, tracing, grounding
Ticket 0734 description makes no mention of creating a [metrics] extra
Partially-verified: the [metrics] extra is described as an intended surface but has no current pyproject.toml or _optional.py entry; evidence class = Unverified
findings.finding: The plan's Description and AC1 explicitly call for documenting the '[metrics]' pip extra, but no such extra exists in pyproject.toml or in _optional.py's EXTRAS registry. Ticket 0734 (the named content prerequisite) also does not create this extra — it focuses on wiring metric specs to the analyzer. Documenting 'pip install nava-rebar[metrics]' or referencing guard_import(..., extra='metrics') would describe a non-existent installation surface.
findings.location: Description: 'the optional [metrics] extra'; AC1: 'docs/reuse-surface.md metrics section: analyzer seam + [metrics] extra + external CLIs'
scenarios: An executor following AC1 writes 'pip install nava-rebar[metrics]' — this extra does not exist and pip would error.
A reader of the new docs tries guard_import(..., extra='metrics') and gets ValueError: unknown extra 'metrics'.
The 0734 content prerequisite lands without creating the extra, leaving docs referencing a phantom installation step.
findings.suggested_fix: If scc and jscpd are external PATH binaries (not pip packages), the docs section should describe them as CLI prerequisites (install via your OS package manager / npm), NOT as a pip extra. Remove '[metrics] extra' from AC1 unless a concrete dependency ticket creates that extra in pyproject.toml + _optional.py.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Upgrade the `relates_to->0734` edge to `depends_on->0734` (or add it as a formal depends_on) to guarantee the `[metrics]` extra and `analyzer.py` exist in the codebase before this docs ticket is executed.
evidence: pyproject.toml [project.optional-dependencies] lists: mcp, reviewbot, ui, agents, eval, tracing, grounding, grounding-t2, dev — no 'metrics' entry
src/rebar/_optional.py EXTRAS dict keys: 'agents', 'eval', 'tracing', 'grounding' — no 'metrics'
Plan: 'Depends on the FINAL shape from 0734 (recorded edge is the non-blocking relates_to->0734; treat 0734 as the content prerequisite)'
0734 ticket dep edges: depends_on 18f9, 21fb, 3b30, 3ba0; relates_to a9dd — 0734 is not a depends_on of this ticket
findings.finding: The plan's AC1 and Description say to document the `[metrics]` extra in docs/reuse-surface.md and docs/user-guide.md, but `[metrics]` does not appear as a key in pyproject.toml `[project.optional-dependencies]` or in `src/rebar/_optional.py`'s `EXTRAS` dict. The creating ticket 0734 is recorded only as `relates_to` (not `depends_on`), so the formal resolution order does not guarantee 0734 lands before this docs ticket, leaving the executor documenting an extra that may not yet exist.
scenarios: Executor arrives at this ticket before 0734 is closed and tries to document a [metrics] extra that doesn't exist yet in pyproject.toml — the documentation would be prospective/inaccurate at merge time.

test_metrics_docs.py extension asserts the [metrics] extra appears in reuse-surface.md, but CI runs against a codebase where pyproject.toml has no such extra — a cross-check test would pass on docs but misrepresent the actual install surface.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the dependency edge from `relates_to` to `depends_on` for ticket 0734, or add 0734 to the formal 'Dependencies' line alongside the note that it is the content prerequisite.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that the 0734 dependency chain (specifically 3ba0) has landed and switched the jscpd invocation to `npx jscpd` before documenting 'jscpd via npx' as the canonical invocation; alternatively, document both modes (PATH binary OR via npx) with detection logic explained.

docs/maintenance-audit-runbook.md:65: 'npx jscpd ...' — npx form exists in runbook but not in the live metric code

Evidence class: Partially-verified — npx invocation is stated intent in related docs but the actual metric code path uses bare jscpd
findings.finding: The plan says to document 'jscpd via npx' as the invocation mode, but the current codebase's only jscpd caller (scripts/backfill_complexity_clone_snapshots.py:72) invokes bare ['jscpd', ...], not ['npx', 'jscpd', ...]. The npx-based invocation is the target mandated by adapter ticket 3ba0, which is a dep of 0734. If this docs ticket is implemented before 3ba0/0734 fully land, the documented CLI invocation pattern will not match the actual code path.
findings.location: Description: 'external-CLI prerequisites (scc, jscpd via npx)'; AC1: 'docs/reuse-surface.md metrics section: analyzer seam + [metrics] extra + external CLIs'
scenarios: Executor writes 'npx jscpd' as the documented prerequisite check; adopters run `which jscpd` but jscpd is installed only via npx, causing confusion about PATH vs npx detection.
Docs describe 'npx jscpd' but the running adapter still calls bare 'jscpd' — mismatched docs create support burden.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'docs lint clean' with a concrete proving command (e.g., `markdownlint docs/reuse-surface.md docs/config.md docs/user-guide.md` or whichever tool the project uses), or cite the AGENTS.md section that specifies it.
evidence: 'docs lint clean' — no command, no tool name, no flags
No AGENTS.md or project convention is cited that would make the lint tool inferrable
findings.finding: The Verification section says 'docs lint clean' but provides no concrete proving command. An executor cannot tell which lint tool to run (markdownlint, mdformat, vale, a custom script) or how to invoke it, so the check cannot be mechanically reproduced.
scenarios: Different executors run different lint tools and reach contradictory pass/fail results
CI uses a specific tool the executor didn't run, causing a late failure

evidence: vague/subjective terms present: clean
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate at least the key assertion targets for the test extension (e.g., presence of '[metrics]' extra name, 'scc'/'jscpd' tool names, '[code_health]' key list, and 'Unavailable' fallback wording) so the extension criterion is outcome-observable, not effort-framed.
evidence: 'EXTEND it, do not break the existing assertions' — the plan names the constraint (don't break) but not the positive specification (what new contract to assert)
No enumeration of which new doc strings, headings, or key names the test should anchor on
findings.finding: The AC 'tests/scripts/test_metrics_docs.py extended' does not specify what new assertions must be added — it only says 'existing assertions still pass'. An executor must independently decide which new doc content to assert (e.g., presence of [code_health] key names, 'Unavailable' fallback prose, [metrics] extra name, scc/jscpd mentions). This defers a real design decision to the executor and makes the criterion effort-framed rather than outcome-observable.
findings.location: Acceptance criteria — 'tests/scripts/test_metrics_docs.py extended (existing assertions still pass)'
scenarios: An executor adds a single trivial assertion (e.g., file exists) and the AC is technically satisfied with no meaningful coverage gain
A reviewer cannot judge whether the extension is sufficient because no target assertions are named
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sub-bullet under the AC: 'New assertions must cover at minimum: (a) `[metrics]` extra name appears in reuse-surface.md; (b) `scc` and `jscpd` CLI names appear in user-guide.md; (c) each `[code_health]` key name appears in config.md; (d) the string `Unavailable` appears in user-guide.md Metrics section.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a lightweight proving command for the AGENTS.md AC, e.g., `grep -n 'metrics' AGENTS.md` to confirm the pointer line exists and references the correct section, or include it in test_metrics_docs.py.
evidence: No grep or diff command is given to assert the new pointer text
The condition ('if the one-liner changed') is checkable in-session but the post-condition is unspecified
findings.finding: The AC 'AGENTS.md metrics pointer updated if the one-liner changed' is conditional but provides no proving command to verify the pointer is correct after the update. A reviewer has no way to confirm the right content is in AGENTS.md beyond a manual diff.
findings.location: Acceptance criteria — 'AGENTS.md metrics pointer updated if the one-liner changed'
scenarios: Executor updates AGENTS.md with stale text; no test catches it
The condition is satisfied but the new pointer points to the wrong section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Align the terminology in AC1 ('analyzer seam') with the Description ('analyzer/adapter set') — or add a parenthetical clarifying they refer to the same thing — so implementers document the correct abstraction consistently.
evidence: Description: 'the analyzer/adapter set'
AC1: 'docs/reuse-surface.md metrics section: analyzer seam + [metrics] extra + external CLIs'
These appear to be the same surface but are named differently
findings.finding: Terminology drift between Description and AC1: the plan uses 'analyzer/adapter set' in the Description and 'analyzer seam' in AC1 for what appears to be the same concept. A reader implementing AC1 may not know whether 'analyzer seam' means the adapter set, the abstract base class, or something narrower.
findings.location: Description ('analyzer/adapter set') vs AC line 1 ('analyzer seam')
scenarios: Implementer writes about the adapter set in reuse-surface.md but test_metrics_docs.py (extended assertions) checks for 'analyzer seam' wording, causing a mismatch or confusion about what to document.
findings.suggested_fix: In AC1, change 'analyzer seam' to 'analyzer/adapter set (the analyzer seam)' or update the Description to use 'analyzer seam' consistently throughout.

There is no existing callable to document, and the anti-drift gate tests/unit/test_reuse_surface_doc.py — which introspects documented callables in reuse-surface.md — is not listed in 'Files likely touched', leaving it as an unenumerated consumer that would break if new callable signatures are added to §5.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile docs/config.md scope: either add it to parent 121b's plan (and re-review 121b), or re-parent this leaf to an epic that contains the [code_health] config docs deliverable (e.g., 0734's parent 839b).
evidence: Parent 121b file_impact lists only: examples/agent-skills/rebar-janitor/phases/discovery.md, docs/user-guide.md, docs/reuse-surface.md, AGENTS.md — docs/config.md is absent.
Parent 121b ACs make no mention of docs/config.md or [code_health] config keys.
The [code_health] config section originates in 0734's parent epic (839b), not in 121b.
findings.finding: The leaf mandates content in docs/config.md ('[code_health]' keys/types/defaults) — a file and content category absent from parent 121b's plan, ACs, and file_impact list.
scenarios: An implementer delivers docs/config.md [code_health] content as directed by the leaf, but the parent's plan-review attestation never covered this deliverable — creating an unreviewed scope expansion.
A future auditor sees docs/config.md modified under 121b's tree and cannot trace the authorization back to any parent AC.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either extend parent 121b's reuse-surface AC to explicitly include the [metrics] extra and external CLI prerequisites, or move that sub-deliverable to a ticket under 0734's parent (839b) where the analyzer/adapter scope is defined.
evidence: Parent 121b reuse-surface AC: 'docs/reuse-surface.md references the reusable rebar.metrics subsystem (the MetricSpec/REGISTRY declarative registry + is_authoritative segregation helper) so library consumers discover it.' — no mention of a [metrics] pip extra or scc/jscpd CLI prerequisites.

Parent 121b's 'What' section: 'reference the metrics surface from the project's canonical documentation' and 'reference the reusable rebar.metrics subsystem' — scoped to the registry abstraction, not installation prerequisites.
The [metrics] extra and scc/jscpd prerequisites come from 0734's adapter/analyzer machinery (a separate epic), not from 121b's registry-discoverability goal.
findings.finding: The leaf's docs/reuse-surface.md AC adds '[metrics] extra + external CLIs (scc, jscpd via npx)' — prerequisite tooling documentation that does not appear anywhere in parent 121b's plan or ACs, which scope only the rebar.metrics registry subsystem (MetricSpec/REGISTRY/is_authoritative).
findings.location: Acceptance criteria: 'docs/reuse-surface.md metrics section: analyzer seam + [metrics] extra + external CLIs'
scenarios: The leaf documents scc/jscpd as prerequisites in docs/reuse-surface.md, but 121b's plan-review attestation only authorized rebar.metrics registry documentation — the tooling prereqs are out-of-bounds for 121b's scope.
If 0734 changes which external CLIs are needed, the leaf's docs diverge from 0734's implementation with no link to 0734's review chain.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a short decision-rationale note (inline in reuse-surface.md or a new ADR entry) explaining WHY Unavailable is the guaranteed fallback (rather than an exception or partial result) so future maintainers understand the contract is intentional and load-bearing.
evidence: Plan description: 'the hard Unavailable fallback contract'
AC2: 'no analyzer -> Unavailable behavior' — describes WHAT, not WHY
AGENTS.md AC3 update is scoped to 'if the one-liner changed', not a rationale capture
Absence: no ADR or design-rationale section is named in 'Files likely touched'
findings.finding: The plan documents WHAT the 'hard Unavailable fallback contract' is and WHERE it applies, but does not call for capturing the decision rationale (why Unavailable rather than an exception, a zero/null, or a partial result) in an ADR or dedicated design-rationale section.
findings.location: Acceptance criteria AC2 / docs/user-guide.md Metrics section / docs/reuse-surface.md
scenarios: A future maintainer wonders why the system returns Unavailable rather than raising or returning zeros, finds no rationale, and inadvertently breaks the contract when adding a new analyzer.
A new consumer of the API is unsure whether Unavailable is a permanent guarantee or an implementation detail they should not rely on.
findings.suggested_fix: In docs/reuse-surface.md, add a 'Design rationale' subsection under the Unavailable fallback contract that states: (a) the guarantee (any missing/erroring analyzer returns Unavailable, never raises), (b) why (uniform consumer error handling, composability), and (c) how to extend it correctly. Alternatively, create docs/adr/NNN-metrics-unavailable-fallback.md and link it from reuse-surface.md.
